description: Israeli legal corpus for pretrial detention bias audit, containing statutes, case law, and guidelines relevant to the Criminal Procedure (Enforcement Powers – Detention) Law and related legal frameworks.

documents.title: Criminal Procedure (Enforcement Powers – Detention) Law, 5756-1996
documents.title_he: חוק סדר הדין הפלילי (סמכויות אכיפה – מעצרים), התשנ"ו-1996
documents.description: Primary Israeli legislation governing arrests and detention in criminal proceedings. Covers police arrest powers, judicial detention orders, detention until end of proceedings, grounds for detention, alternatives to detention, time limits, and judicial review. Reconstructed from official sources and legal databases.
sections: Chapter 4: Detention Until End of Proceedings (Sections 16-24)
Schedule: Offenses Carrying Presumption of Dangerousness
key_sections_for_audit: Section 21 — Grounds for Detention Until End of Proceedings
Section 21A — Duty to Consider Alternatives to Detention

documents.title: חוק סדר הדין הפלילי (סמכויות אכיפה – מעצרים), התשנ"ו-1996
documents.description: Hebrew text of the primary Israeli detention law, including definitions, police arrest powers, court-ordered detention, detention until end of proceedings, grounds for detention, alternatives to detention, and appeals. Reconstructed from official sources.
sections: פרק ג': מעצר על פי צו בית משפט

documents.title: Basic Law: Human Dignity and Freedom
documents.description: Israel's quasi-constitutional Basic Law protecting human dignity and freedom. Contains the right to personal liberty (Section 5), the limitation clause (Section 8) establishing the proportionality test, and protections for life, body, dignity, property, and privacy. Includes detailed commentary on relevance to detention law.
sections: Section 2 — Preservation of Life, Body, and Dignity
Section 4 — Protection of Life, Body, and Dignity
Relevance to Pretrial Detention Bias Audit
key_sections_for_audit: Section 5 — Personal Liberty (right against deprivation of liberty)
Relevance to Pretrial Detention Bias Audit (equal application, alternatives)

documents.title: Israeli Bail and Conditional Release Provisions
documents.description: Comprehensive compilation of Israeli bail and conditional release provisions, synthesized from the Detention Law, Electronic Monitoring Law (2014), Conditional Release from Imprisonment Law (2001), and related regulations. Covers financial bail, house arrest, electronic monitoring, reporting requirements, and all forms of alternative conditions.
sections: Part 1: Overview of Conditional Release Framework (Sections 1-3)
Part 6: Enforcement and Consequences of Violation (Sections 24-25)
key_sections_for_audit: Part 2 — Financial Bail (amount determination, socioeconomic impact)
Part 3 — House Arrest (custodian requirements, residence suitability)

documents.title: Key Supreme Court Precedents on Pretrial Detention in Israel
documents.title_he: פסקי דין מנחים של בית המשפט העליון בנושא מעצר טרום-משפטי
documents.court: Supreme Court of Israel (High Court of Justice / Criminal Appeals)
documents.description: Compilation of 10 landmark Supreme Court decisions on pretrial detention, covering proportionality, alternatives to detention, presumption of dangerousness, due process, evidentiary standards, bail equality, and dignity in detention. Each case includes citation, facts, holding, key principles, and relevance to bias audit.
sections: Case 1: HCJ 6055/95 Tzemach v. Minister of Defense (Proportionality)
Case 2: CrimA 7048/97 Anonymous v. State (Alternatives Examination)
Case 3: CrimA 8087/03 State v. Anonymous (DV Presumption)
Case 4: HCJ 2028/08 Public Committee v. Minister of Justice (Due Process)
Case 5: HCJ 7146/12 Adam v. Knesset (Individualized Assessment)
Case 6: CrimA 2581/95 Ganimat v. State (Evidentiary Standard)
Case 7: CrimA 4424/98 Anonymous v. State (Combined Alternatives)
Case 8: CrimA 6352/10 State v. Anonymous (Duration Limits)
Case 9: FH CrimA 10141/09 Ben Haim v. State (Bail Equality)
Case 10: HCJ 5100/94 Public Committee v. State (Dignity in Detention)
Summary Table: Key Precedents and Relevance

documents.title: Guidelines for Pretrial Detention Hearings in Israel
documents.description: Comprehensive practical guidelines for conducting pretrial detention hearings in Israel. Covers constitutional framework, types of hearings, substantive requirements (three-stage analysis), procedural requirements, structured decision-making framework, factors for risk assessment, bias awareness, special populations, and statistical indicators for bias monitoring.
source_references: Basic Law: Human Dignity and Freedom, 5752-1992
sections: Part 3: What the Judge Must Consider (Section 9 — Three-Stage Analysis)
Part 6: Avoiding Bias in Detention Decisions (Sections 16-18)
Part 9: Statistical Indicators for Bias Monitoring (Sections 26-27)
key_sections_for_audit: Section 9 — Three-Stage Analysis (prima facie, grounds, alternatives)
Section 15 — Factors Relevant to Risk Assessment
Section 27 — Red Flags for Potential Bias

chunking_guidance.notes: Documents are formatted with clear section headers using '=' delimiters and 'Section NN.' / 'סעיף NN.' format to facilitate automatic chunking. Each section is self-contained and includes sufficient context for standalone retrieval.
